wrapUp: The Round of 32 closes on 3 July with Argentina, Australia, Egypt, Colombia and Ghana chasing the final last-16 places, while Spain and Portugal have already set up a blockbuster Iberian derby in Dallas on 6 July after Mikel Oyarzabal's double helped La Roja cruise past Austria and Goncalo Ramos snatched a stoppage-time winner against Croatia amid late VAR drama. Co-hosts USA are through to face Belgium in Seattle but will be without Folarin Balogun after his red card against Bosnia, while Youri Tielemans converted the latest goal in World Cup history to send Belgium past Senegal in extra time. England and Mexico await their Azteca showdown on 6 July as Harry Kane's late rescue against DR Congo and the hosts' clean-sheet run remain the defining subplots in the other half of the bracket. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 3 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Williams and Pino still out as Spain set up Portugal clash
stories.summary: Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino both missed Spain's 3-0 Round of 32 win over Austria on 2 July and remain doubts for the last-16 meeting with Portugal in Dallas on 6 July. Luis de la Fuente leaned on Mikel Oyarzabal, who scored twice, and Pedro Porro from right-back as La Roja kept a fourth straight clean sheet, but the absence of both wide men leaves Alex Baena and a returning Lamine Yamal to cover the flanks against Roberto Martinez's side.

stories.headline: Balogun suspended as USA advance to face Belgium
stories.summary: Folarin Balogun scored his third goal of the tournament to put the United States ahead against Bosnia and Herzegovina on 2 July, but was sent off in the 64th minute after VAR reviewed a challenge on Tarik Muharemovic and will miss the last-16 tie with Belgium in Seattle on 6 July. Malik Tillman's free-kick sealed a 2-0 win for the ten-man co-hosts, ending a long run without victory over European opposition at World Cups, but Mauricio Pochettino must reshuffle his attack without his leading scorer.

stories.headline: England miss James and Quansah at right-back before Mexico tie
stories.summary: Reece James remained sidelined with a hamstring injury and Jarrell Quansah twisted an ankle in the Panama win as England entered their Round of 32 fixture without either of their preferred right-backs. Thomas Tuchel said both were getting closer to fitness but needed England to advance before they could return, leaving Djed Spence to cover the position against DR Congo. James was spotted speaking with Jude Bellingham during a hydration break in Atlanta, underlining how thin England's defensive options remain before the Mexico tie at the Estadio Azteca on 6 July.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Ramos and Ronaldo send Portugal past Croatia into Spain tie
stories.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo converted a 68th-minute penalty for his first World Cup knockout goal as Portugal came from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in Toronto on 2 July, before Goncalo Ramos headed a stoppage-time winner from Rafael Leao's cross. Ivan Perisic had given Croatia the lead in the 53rd minute in what was the first World Cup match featuring two outfield players aged 40 or over, but Roberto Martinez's substitutions proved decisive and Portugal now face Spain in Dallas on 6 July.

stories.headline: Tielemans penalty in 125th minute sends Belgium past Senegal
stories.summary: Belgium trailed Senegal 2-0 with five minutes of regulation time remaining before Romelu Lukaku and Youri Tielemans forced extra time, and Tielemans then converted a VAR-awarded penalty in the 125th minute to complete a 3-2 win in Seattle on 1 July. Rudi Garcia's bold decision to withdraw Kevin De Bruyne and Jeremy Doku with his side facing elimination proved pivotal, and Belgium now face co-hosts USA in the last 16 on 6 July after the latest goal in World Cup history.

stories.headline: Kane double rescues England and sets up Mexico last-16 tie
stories.summary: Harry Kane headed Anthony Gordon's cross to equalise in the 75th minute and drilled a fierce strike into the roof of the net in the 86th to complete a 2-1 comeback against DR Congo in Atlanta on 1 July. Brian Cipenga had given the Leopards a shock seventh-minute lead, and Lionel Mpasi denied England repeatedly before Tuchel's side finally broke through. England will face co-hosts Mexico at the Estadio Azteca on 6 July, a fixture that will test whether the Three Lions can raise their level after a laboured knockout debut.

stories.headline: Mexico beat Ecuador to set up England last-16 tie
stories.summary: Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez scored either side of half-time as co-hosts Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 at the Estadio Azteca on 1 July to reach the last 16 without conceding at the tournament. Kick-off was delayed by an hour because of storms in Mexico City, and Piero Hincapie was sent off in stoppage time after covering his mouth during an altercation with Santiago Gimenez. Mexico's reward is a home last-16 tie with England on 6 July, with altitude and crowd support giving Javier Aguirre's side a genuine edge against a team that struggled to break down DR Congo.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Final Round of 32 slate set as Argentina headline Friday fixtures
stories.summary: Three win-or-go-home ties on 3 July complete the first knockout round of the expanded 48-team format, with Australia facing Egypt in Arlington, Argentina meeting Cabo Verde in Miami and Colombia taking on Ghana in Kansas City. The winners join a last-16 field already featuring an Iberian derby between Spain and Portugal, a co-host showdown between USA and Belgium, and England against Mexico at altitude. Kick-off times across North American venues mean some teams face short turnarounds before the Round of 16 begins on 4 July.

stories.headline: Croatia fans protest after Gvardiol goal disallowed in Toronto
stories.summary: Portugal's 2-1 win over Croatia on 2 July ended in chaos when Josko Gvardiol thought he had equalised in the 113th minute, only for VAR to rule the goal out after detecting a faint touch from Igor Matanovic on Ivan Perisic's cross that left Mario Pasalic in an offside position. Croatian supporters threw bottles onto the pitch and whistled in protest during a lengthy on-field review, and the incident was the tenth goal overturned by VAR at this tournament. Portugal advance to face Spain but the controversy adds to growing debate about connected-ball technology and knockout-stage officiating.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Oyarzabal double sends Spain past Austria without conceding
stories.summary: Mikel Oyarzabal scored in the 36th and 89th minutes and Pedro Porro headed a second-half goal as Spain beat Austria 3-0 on 2 July at SoFi Stadium, their first World Cup knockout win since the 2010 final. Luis de la Fuente's side have not conceded in four matches and goalkeeper Unai Simon has faced limited work, while Marc Cucurella supplied assists on both Oyarzabal strikes. Spain join Mexico as the only teams yet to let in a goal and now face Portugal in a last-16 tie that could define the European path to the final.

stories.headline: Switzerland end 88-year knockout drought against Algeria
stories.summary: Breel Embolo and Dan Ndoye scored either side of half-time as Switzerland beat Algeria 2-0 in Vancouver on 2 July, their first World Cup knockout victory since beating Germany in 1938. Johan Manzambi's solo run set up the opener in the 10th minute and Murat Yakin's side controlled a tie in which Algeria managed just 0.73 expected goals. Switzerland will face the winner of Colombia and Ghana in the last 16 on 7 July, having reached the knockout stage for a fourth consecutive tournament.

stories.headline: Mbappe brace sends France past Sweden into Paraguay tie
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe scored in each half as France beat Sweden 3-0 on 30 June at MetLife Stadium, with Bradley Barcola adding the second after Michael Olise's creative prompting. Mbappe moved level with Lionel Messi on six goals at this tournament and became the all-time leading scorer in World Cup knockout matches with 10, while France registered 25 shots in their most dominant display of the competition so far. Didier Deschamps's side face Paraguay in Philadelphia on 4 July, with Les Bleus now widely regarded as the team to beat after a third straight World Cup in which they have scored three or more in consecutive matches.

stories.headline: Haaland winner gives Norway first World Cup knockout victory
stories.summary: Erling Haaland prodded home from close range in the 86th minute as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on 30 June in Dallas to reach the last 16 for the first time in their history. The Manchester City striker has five goals at the tournament, and Stale Solbakken's side will face Brazil in New Jersey on 5 July after surviving a second half in which the Elephants pushed hard for an equaliser. Norway's path now runs through the form team of the knockout stage so far, with Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil having needed stoppage-time heroics to see off Japan.
